message:
fix(ffs-core): cap ext4 read buffers to (file_size - offset) before allocation (bd-f44ac)

The three ext4 read entry points in `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs` —
`OpenFs::read_file` (extent + non-extent path), `Ext4FsOps::read`
(`FsOps` impl on `Ext4FsOps`), and `OpenFs as FsOps`'s extent-aware
read branch — all allocated the caller-requested `size` *before*
applying any EOF / file-size truncation:

    let mut buf = vec![0_u8; size as usize];
    let n = self.read_file_data(...)?;
    buf.truncate(n);

That meant a request of `u32::MAX` bytes against a file with only a
few bytes left past `offset` would still allocate a ~4 GiB buffer,
zero it, perform a tiny read, and immediately truncate it. On 32-bit
targets it could outright OOM, and on 64-bit hosts it caused
unnecessary RSS spikes and avoidable allocator pressure on the FUSE
read path. The post-truncate result was correct, but the allocation
was unbounded.

Adds a single shared helper:

    fn ext4_read_buffer_len(
        file_size: u64,
        offset: u64,
        requested: u32,
    ) -> ffs_error::Result<usize>

Behavior:

  - If `offset >= file_size` or `requested == 0`, return `Ok(0)` —
    the read trivially yields zero bytes; no buffer needed.
  - Otherwise compute `(file_size - offset).min(requested as u64)`;
    that is the maximum number of bytes the read could legitimately
    return without overrunning either the request or EOF.
  - `usize::try_from(bounded)` to safely degrade to a `Corruption`
    error on platforms where the bounded length cannot be addressed
    (32-bit targets reading from a >4 GiB file). The error carries
    a clear `"ext4 read length {bounded} exceeds addressable memory"`
    message so it surfaces distinctly in logs.

All three call sites are migrated to:

    let mut buf = vec![0_u8; ext4_read_buffer_len(inode.size, offset, size)?];

The post-read `buf.truncate(n)` calls are kept where they previously
existed: short reads from the underlying extent / indirect path can
still legally return less than the capped length, so the truncation
is still load-bearing.

Tests added in the `tests` module (using the existing
`build_ext4_image_with_extents` fixture, whose payload is
`b"Hello, extent!"`, 14 bytes):

  - `read_file_oversized_tail_request_is_capped_before_allocation` —
    requests `u32::MAX` bytes at offset 13 (one byte from EOF),
    expects exactly `b"!"`. Pre-fix this would attempt a
    4 GiB allocation; post-fix it allocates 1 byte.
  - `read_file_oversized_eof_request_returns_empty_without_allocation` —
    requests `u32::MAX` bytes at offset 14 (exactly EOF), expects
    an empty result and the helper short-circuits to `Ok(0)`.
  - `open_fs_fsops_ext4_read_oversized_tail_request_is_capped` —
    same semantics but exercised through the `dyn FsOps` trait
    object on `OpenFs`, covering the third call site.

`.beads/issues.jsonl` is updated in the same commit to record the
in-progress `bd-f44ac` task this change implements, keeping the
issue store synced with the code edit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
